title: BibleDesk
tagline: Offline-first Bible reader with verse tagging and study tools.
description: BibleDesk is a desktop Bible reader built for serious study — offline-first, with verse tagging, cross-reference linking, and a clean distraction-free reading mode. No accounts, no cloud dependency.
story: Built because every Bible app I tried either needed an account, pushed notifications, or loaded ads between chapters. BibleDesk is the reader I wanted: local, fast, and focused on the text.
journey: BibleDesk came from a straightforward frustration — I wanted a Bible reader that worked offline, didn't require an account, and stayed out of the way. Most apps in the space are built around engagement metrics, not reading. Building it meant solving the offline-first data problem first, then layering study tools on top once the core reading experience was solid.
forWho: Readers, students, and researchers who want a focused, offline-capable Bible study tool without account walls or distractions.

detailedSpecs.architecture: Electron desktop app. SQLite stores the full Bible text locally. No network dependency after install.
